title: RapidMeta Psychiatry | Zuranolone for Postpartum Depression v1.0

pico.pop: Adults aged 18-45 with postpartum depression (DSM-5; major depressive episode <=12 months postpartum) and HAM-D-17 >=26
pico.int: Zuranolone 50 mg orally once daily for 14 days (neuroactive steroid GABA-A allosteric modulator)
pico.out: Change in HAM-D-17 score from baseline at day 15 (primary)
pico.subgroup: Severity at baseline (HAM-D 26-30 vs >=31), prior antidepressant use, breastfeeding status

real_data_entries.name: SKYLARK
real_data_entries.group: Postpartum depression, zuranolone 50 mg PO QD x 14 days vs placebo (HAM-D-17 day-15 change)

allOutcomes.title: Change in HAM-D-17 from baseline at day 15 (zuranolone 50 mg vs placebo MD; primary)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Deligiannidis KM et al. Am J Psychiatry 2023;180:668-675 (SKYLARK).

real_data_entries.name: ROBIN
real_data_entries.group: Severe postpartum depression, zuranolone 30 mg PO QD x 14 days vs placebo (HAM-D-17 day-15 change; lower-dose pivotal)

allOutcomes.title: Change in HAM-D-17 from baseline at day 15 (zuranolone 30 mg vs placebo MD)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Deligiannidis KM et al. JAMA Psychiatry 2021;78:951-959 (ROBIN).
